title: Lemon cupcakes
subtitle: Zesty lemon sponge with lemon buttercream
excerpt: A light lemon sponge with zest worked into the batter, topped with a sharp lemon buttercream. More lemony than a Victoria sponge; a little less sweet than the American original.
sourceNotes: The lemon cupcake is a standard of American bakeries adapted for British tastes, which typically prefer less sweetness in the frosting and a stronger citrus note in the sponge. This recipe synthesises the British Victoria sponge proportions with the American cupcake format, adjusting sugar downward in the buttercream relative to the Hummingbird Bakery original (Tim Malouf, 2009).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze un-iced sponges in a sealed bag for up to 2 months. Ice after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Sponges keep for 1 day in an airtight tin. Ice on the day of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: lemon buttercream piped in a swirl, lemon zest to finish

content.text: The lemon flavour in these cupcakes comes from two places: zest rubbed into the sugar (which extracts the fragrant oils) and lemon juice in the buttercream. Both matter. Zest alone gives aroma but little sharpness; juice alone gives acid but can turn the buttercream grainy. Together they give the full lemon profile.

Use unwaxed lemons. Waxed lemon zest contains a bitter resin coating that comes through in the baked sponge. If you can only find waxed lemons, scrub them vigorously in hot water and dry before zesting.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Line a 12-hole muffin tin with paper cases. Rub the {{lemon}} zest into the {{caster-sugar}} with your fingertips until the sugar smells strongly of lemon. Beat in the {{unsalted-butter}} until pale and fluffy. Add the {{eggs}} one at a time, beating after each. Fold in the {{plain-flour}} and {{baking-powder}}, then stir in the {{whole-milk}}. Divide evenly between the cases. Bake for 16 to 18 minutes until the sponges spring back when lightly pressed. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth and pale. Add the {{icing-sugar}} in two additions, beating well. Add the {{lemon-juice}} and beat for 3 minutes until the buttercream is fluffy and sharp. If it is too stiff, add a few drops more lemon juice. Pipe or spread onto the cooled cupcakes and scatter a little {{lemon}} zest over the top.
